channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
item.description: AI agents are accelerating the growth of non-human identities, making it harder for organizations to understand what exists, who owns it, and what it can access. Netwrix explains why stronger visibili...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fresh ATM Crypto Software Bugs: Jackpot or Bust?
item.description: Organizations, and possibly ATMs, are at risk of compromise, thanks to holes in a Microsoft BitLocker security wrapper....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
item.description:
The China-linked cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been attributed to a new Rust-based remote access trojan (RAR) called MODBEACON.
Chinese cybersecurity company QiAnXin said that while the th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI Coding: Do Security Risks Outweigh Productivity Gains?
item.description: AI coding tools cost $19-$200/month/user, but security scanning, remediation, and false positives add hidden costs. Are the productivity gains worth it?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description:
A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch.
FoxIO resea...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
item.description: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: From 17,000 to 1.1 Million Assets: How Lumen Technologies Rebuilt Exposure Management at Scale
item.description: Most enterprises assume their asset inventory is close enough to accurate. The evidence suggests otherwise. According to a survey of over 600 security leaders in the 2026 Axonius Actionability Report,...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
item.description: A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists naming mo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Study of 281 Free Android VPN Apps Finds Traffic Leaks, Unencrypted Data, and Tracking
item.description: Researchers ran 281 of the most popular free VPN apps on the Google Play Store through a new testing system and found that many fail at the basics people install a VPN for, i.e., keeping their traffic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
item.description: A threat actor has been targeting organizations spanning multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that prompt Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey with an aim to carry out...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain Over $5 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words that...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
item.description: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
item.description: A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware operator...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenMandriva Linux says contributor tried to sabotage the project
item.description: The OpenMandriva Linux project announced that it was the target of an attempted act of internal sabotage after a dispute among contributors. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran's Cyber Crosshairs Focus Beyond Critical Infrastructure
item.description: Obscurity isn't a defense. If your company has any Internet-facing vulnerability, you're at risk from multiple threats....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Reins in RoguePlanet Zero-Day Threat
item.description: The researcher known as "Nightmare-Eclipse" published a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for the Windows Defender vulnerability in early June after dropping several other Microsoft zero-days....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Injective SDK on npm infected with cryptocurrency wallet stealer
item.description: Hackers compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and used it to publish a malicious package on the Node Package Manager (npm) that stole cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mn...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity & Most Organizations Aren't Ready
item.description: If you're handling them like a service account or API token, consider yourself behind. AI agents need a fundamentally different approach....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description:
Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API.
"Operat...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
item.description: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePo...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft expects more Windows security updates from AI-discovered flaws
item.description: Microsoft says Windows users should expect to see an increase in security updates as the company increasingly relies on artificial intelligence to discover vulnerabilities in its codebase. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description: GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor authenticatio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description:
Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it.
This week is full of that kind of...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure genera...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: As Global Conflicts Go Digital, Businesses Need Wartime Gameplans
item.description: The fate of a Ukrainian tax software company shows how modern cyberwarfare can claim casualties far beyond the battlefield, and how businesses across the ocean still need to protect themselves....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Reduced Summer IT Coverage
item.description: Security operations don't slow down when IT teams take vacation, but staffing levels often do. Kaseya explains how AI-driven automation can help organizations maintain consistent security operations a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
item.description: A cryptomining incident highlights how AI gateways can provide access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and identity and access management (IAM) data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description: AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the ne...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft to retire the OWA Light client in Exchange Server
item.description: Microsoft has announced plans to disable Outlook Web Access (OWA) Light, the lightweight version of the Outlook Web App email client, in a future Exchange Server update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
